stories.title: Man who was killed by ICE in Texas never ‘weaponized’ his vehicle, witnesses say
stories.summary: Lawyer says men who were riding in van are being pressured to self-deport as footage of incident is reportedly with investigators Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The three men who were in the van when federal immigration officials shot and killed a man in Houston, Texas , this week are strongly disputing the Trump administration ’s narrative of the events and are being pressured to sign deportation orders, according to their lawyer and lawmakers. The three men, who were arreste

stories.title: Spain shrugs off Trump’s claim it caved on defense spending
stories.summary: Madrid is rejecting the notion that it has bowed to U.S. President Donald Trump’s demand to increase its military spending. During this week’s NATO summit in Ankara, Trump took aim at Spain for refusing to agree that all of the alliance’s members devote 5 percent of gross domestic product to defense over the next decade. After lambasting Madrid for being a “terrible partner,” the U.S. president on Wednesday instructed Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to “cut o

stories.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
stories.summary: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

stories.title: How a fake presidential council ended up with a budget of almost $1m in Nigeria
stories.summary: The government says it was set up with a forged letter of appointment but others say there is more to it.

stories.title: Was Erdoğan's gun gift a faux pas or an old-school diplomatic tradition?
stories.summary: When NATO leaders opened their gift boxes from the Turkish president and found a .357 Magnum revolver, some panicked and others laughed. But gifting heads of state lavish, personalised firearms is a tradition as old as Samuel Colt — and as recent as a Czech pistol presented to Trump in 2019.

stories.title: Senior Democrat backs Trump’s call for more European defense spending
stories.summary: Rahm Emanuel, one of the Democratic Party’s 2028 presidential hopefuls, says Donald Trump is right to demand that Europe spend more on defense. In an interview with POLITICO’s Gordon Repinski in Berlin, the former Chicago mayor said Europe had become too reliant on America’s resources within the NATO military alliance. “You’re responsible for getting dependent on America over the years,” Emanuel said, referring to the European members of NATO. “The truth is we’re responsible for letting yo

stories.title: Takarítóként hétezer dollárt talált egy velencei vaporettón, egy furcsa olasz szabály miatt mind megtarthatta
stories.summary: De éveket kellett várnia rá, sőt még a bíróságon is küzdött érte.

stories.title: Kifutott a Tisza-trade, de hosszabb távon még vonzó lehet a forint és a magyar tőzsde
stories.summary: Rövid távon valószínűleg learatták már a magyar eszközökön elérhető profit javát a befektetők, de ha sikerül befutni a kormány által felvázolt gazdasági pályát, akkor lesznek még lehetőségek.

stories.title: Norvégiában a vb miatt mindenki közös evezőre jutott
stories.summary: A brazilok elleni győzelem nemzeti eufóriáig fokozta a focilázat az országban. A koronaherceg együtt evezett a néppel, hiánycikk lett a piros, kék és fehér pamutfonal, és közben a norvégok szavakat keresnek arra, amit éreznek éppen. Bármeddig jut Norvégia, már győztesnek érezheti magát.

stories.title: Közel 10 milliárd forint ment határon túli focikluboknak a választás előtt
stories.summary: Bár a hivatalos cél a magyar identitás mélyítése, rezsire, fizetésekre, étkeztetésre és nyomtatásra is költöttek a támogatásból.

stories.title: Béres Attila: Az Operettszínház vezetése dilettáns, ezt bátran bemondom, pereljetek be
stories.summary: A miskolci színház igazgatója Veiszer Alindának azt mondta, nem ugrana el a Nemzeti Színház igazgatói pályázata elől, miközben élesen bírálta a Fidesz politikai kinevezettjeit.

stories.title: Magyar Péter megüzente Sulyok Tamásnak, mi fog történni, ha nem írja alá az Alaptörvény-módosítást
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök azt írta, Sulyoknak öt napja lesz aláírni a módosítást, utána megindítják ellene a megfosztási eljárást.

stories.title: Kiemelkedő pillanatok a demokráciáért aggódó Orbán Viktortól
stories.summary: Ellenzékből Orbán elkezdett aggódni a jogállamiságért, és sérelmezi az új kormány fideszesekkel szemben tanúsított stílusát. Videónkban megmutatjuk, miniszterelnökként hogyan beszélt azokról és azokhoz, akik nem értettek vele egyet.

stories.title: Newly released footage shows revolver gifted by Erdogan to NATO leaders
stories.summary: Images shared by the Lithuanian Presidency show a revolver given by Turkey's Erdogan to every NATO leader as a parting gift after the summit in Ankara. Each leader received a box containing a revolver engraved with their name, six bullets and a note exempting the weapons from export control.

stories.title: Már-már kihagyhatatlan éjszakai libegőzés, Budapest a festők szemével, Kemény György 90
stories.summary: E heti kulturális ajánlónkban a II. Fonyódi Könyvfesztivál és egy olyan program is helyet kapott, amelyen a tíz éve elhunyt Esterházy Péterre emlékeznek.

stories.title: Már két embert gyanúsít garázdasággal a rendőrség az Orbán Viktor veszprémi országjárásán történtek miatt
stories.summary: A Telex vette videóra a kampányban, hogy két férfi rárontott a kutyapártos tiltakozókra Orbán Viktor országjárása előtt. A rendőrség szerint mindketten panasszal éltek a gyanúsítással szemben.

stories.title: Biztonságosan fogyasztható az itthon kapható tészta, aminek a gyártója külföldön szalmonellafertőzést észlelt
stories.summary: A magyar forgalmazó termékei nem érintettek a fertőzésben.

stories.title: Hungary joins European Public Prosecutor's Office
stories.summary: The EPPO will be able to investigate financial crimes involving the EU budget committed since June 2021 even retroactively, and may bring charges related to them.

stories.title: Szeleczky Ádám lesz átmenetileg a Duna csatornaigazgatója
stories.summary: A riporterként és kreatív producerként ismert szakember a Duna csatornaigazgatója lesz, emellett a hírszolgáltatáson kívüli tévés területért felel az átmeneti időszakban.

stories.title: Annyira nincs víz már a Balatonban sem, hogy rövidíteni kellett a Kékszalag vitorlásverseny útvonalát
stories.summary: Az 55 centis várható átlagos vízállás 60 centivel kevesebb az ideálisnál, és 40 centivel marad el a tavalyitól.

stories.title: Medián: A magyarok 83 százaléka elégedett Magyar Péter országgyűlési beszédeinek tartalmával
stories.summary: A HVG számára készült kutatás szerint a stílust sokan kifogásolják.

stories.title: Súlyos válságban van a Magyar Máltai Szeretetszolgálat a szervezet ügyvezetője szerint
stories.summary: Thaisz Miklós szerint az Unió és a korábbi kormány konfliktusa lehetetlen helyzetbe hozta a szeretetszolgálatot.

stories.title: Tarr Zoltán: Olyanná lett a református gyülekezeteink működése, amilyen a kommunizmus idején volt
stories.summary: A társadalmi kapcsolatokért és kultúráért felelős miniszter szerint attól nem lesz keresztény a politika, hogy állandóan keresztény Magyarországról beszélünk.

stories.title: A benzinhiány szorítja sarokba az orosz propagandát
stories.summary: Háborúpárti bloggerek képviselőkkel, benzinért sorban állók egymással csapnak össze, miközben a Kreml kezelhető nehézségekről és harci fölényről beszél. Az orosz finomítókba csapódó ukrán drónok és a lassan leépülő gazdaság azonban már nem fedhető el sikerpropagandával, ezért a Kreml is változtat eddigi stratégiáján.

stories.title: Magyarország egyre fogy, míg az EU népessége a bevándorlók miatt nő
stories.summary: Egy év alatt 706 ezerrel nőtt az Európai Unió lakosságának száma.

stories.title: Borisz lett az állatkerti manulkölyök, méghozzá II. Borisz
stories.summary: Lehetne akár harmadik Borisz is, akkor is meg kéne zabálni a cukiságot!

stories.title: France fans erupt as Les Bleus down Morocco to reach semis
stories.summary: Scenes of jubilation unfolded in Paris and at Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, as Les Bleus beat Morocco 2-0 on 09 July to secure a place in the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

stories.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
stories.summary: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

stories.title: ‘Death sentence’: Trump’s EPA to open habitats of endangered species to logging and mining
stories.summary: Habitat destruction strongest driver of species loss, with legislation keeping 99% of listed species from going extinct The Trump administration repealed a crucial part of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) on Friday, finalizing a new rule that will open habitats of imperiled wildlife to development, logging, mining and other uses. For the last 50 years, the landmark environmental law included a broader understanding of the word “harm”, which ensured that not just the plants and animals themselves

stories.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

stories.title: Zelenskyy: China urged Putin not to use nuclear weapons in Ukraine
stories.summary: KYIV — China warned Russia against using nuclear weapons in response to Ukrainian strikes on its territory, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told reporters Thursday. “I think you heard such voices in Russian media: ‘What if we respond to Ukrainian strikes with nuclear weapons?’ And it seems to me that this was the first time China … directly responded in an ultimatum-like form — that there can be no thought whatsoever of using nuclear weapons,” Zelenskyy said. The Ukrainian presid

stories.title: Russian authorities ask residents to work from home as fuel crisis escalates
stories.summary: Russia’s Siberian government officials are telling businesses to shift to remote work when possible and limit energy consumption, according to local media reports . “Officials are … advised to plan official trips more selectively and make wider use of remote interaction formats (such as conducting meetings online),” said Olga Krylova, the deputy governor of Siberia’s Tomsk region. In Novosibirsk, another Siberian region, Deputy Governor Konstantin Khalzov signed a d

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy creates ‘long-range impact’ command to strike Russian energy sector
stories.summary: New initiative to focus ‘100%’ on damaging Moscow’s ability to wage war; strikes on Kyiv injure six. What we know on day 1,599 Continue reading...

stories.title: Berlin hostage situation: Man holds woman in supermarket
stories.summary: An hourslong police operation is underway in Berlin, where a man has taken a woman hostage in a supermarket. The man reportedly "pulled out" a big knife before the supermarket closed on Friday evening.

stories.title: US: Trump looked to bypass federal election agency before firings, says report
stories.summary: The White House explored declaring a national emergency to address alleged vulnerabilities in voting machines before Trump ousted leaders of the Election Assistance Commission, a report has found.

stories.title: EU tells Meta to change its apps’ addictive design
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission asked Meta to change the addictive design of its social media platforms Instagram and Facebook or risk fines for breaking the EU’s online child safety rules. The bloc’s executive issued its findings under the Digital Services Act on Friday, telling Meta to disable features like autoplay and infinite scroll in its default settings, to implement effective “screen time breaks” and to tweak its recommendation algorithm that is currently

stories.title: Iran and US to continue talks, but ceasefire is over, Trump says
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump declared on Friday that the ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. is “OVER,” though diplomatic talks will continue, after a tumultuous week in which the two sides traded blows and the president suggested restarting his blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. “The Islamic Republic of Iran has asked us to continue ‘talks.’ We have agreed to do so, but the United States has stated to them, in no uncertain terms, that the Cease Fire is OVER!” he wrote on Truth Social. Iranian for

stories.title: Police investigating death of Ann Widdecombe as murder
stories.summary: LONDON — Police in southwest England said Friday that the death of former Conservative minister Ann Widdecombe will be investigated as a murder. The 78-year-old, a former MEP and staunch Brexiteer who found popularity on reality competition shows, was found dead in her home in Devon on Thursday with evidence of “serious injuries,” according to the Devon and Cornwall police . The police also said that a 26-year-old white British man has been arrested on suspicion of murder, and will r

stories.title: Graham Platner officially withdraws candidacy for US Senate in Maine
stories.summary: Paperwork ends Democratic campaign laden with scandals, including most recently accusations of sexual assault Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee for US Senate in Maine, officially withdrew his candidacy on Friday afternoon, ending a campaign laden with scandals. Maine’s secretary of state confirmed Platner had filed the paperwork to remove his name from the November ballot, two days after Platner publicly said he planned to exit the race. Continue reading...

stories.title: Crews again drain Trump’s troubled reflecting pool
stories.summary: Workers observed draining water from Lincoln Memorial pool plagued by algae blooms and peeling paint Crews were observed Friday draining the plagued Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool, a landmark at the heart of Donald Trump’s mission to beautify Washington DC. Interior secretary Doug Burgum, whose agency oversees the National Park Service, told conservative podcaster Katie Miller in an interview released earlier this week that the new round of draining was planned. He also said that the water mig

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll passes 4,000 as scale of recovery effort looms large
stories.summary: Nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more are listed as missing amid calls by UN and president Delcy Rodríguez for financial help The death toll in Venezuela’s devastating twin earthquakes has topped 4,000, the government said on Friday. At least 4,118 people were killed and 16,740 injured in the back-to-back quakes on 24 June that flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, Venezuelan parliament chief Jorge Rodriguez wrote on Telegram. Thousands more are listed as missing. Cont

stories.title: Peter Falconio murder 25 years on: new footage shows dying Australian outback killer’s refusal to reveal body’s location
stories.summary: NT police release body-worn camera vision of Bradley John Murdoch denying knowing where UK backpacker’s body is weeks before his death Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Infamous killer Bradley John Murdoch aggressively denied knowing where the body of still-missing backpacker Peter Falconio was during a police interview weeks before he died. NT police released body-worn camera vision of the interview on Saturday, days before the 25th anniversary of the killing of the 2

stories.title: Ryanair has axed its family seating policy – but kids’ fees still add up
stories.summary: The airfare for a baby on your lap could cost more than your own ticket. Here’s how airline charges and travel taxes can hit you Ryanair recently stopped making parents pay to sit next to their children but depending on the airline the hidden extra costs involved in flying with children can be substantial. In some cases, you can even end up spending more for the baby on your lap than you paid for your own flight. Your baby might not need a seat, but you are still likely to pay fees for them to t

stories.title: Meta ditches Muse Image AI feature because it ‘misses the mark’ on users’ privacy
stories.summary: Meta was criticised for feature launched on Tuesday that automatically lets users generate images using content from public Instagram accounts Meta has said ⁠it is discontinuing an AI feature launched this week that allowed users to generate images using public Instagram ⁠accounts, after drawing widespread ⁠criticism over ​privacy concerns, including from a Hollywood union. “Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control ⁠over whether their public content could be re

stories.title: Losing our religion? Australia would no longer be majority religious if format of census question changed, survey finds
stories.summary: Ahead of the census in August, campaign group suggests current poll design overstates the nation’s religiousness Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia would no longer be a majority religious country if the format of a question in the census was changed, according to a new survey. The Essential Media poll tested the existing census format, where people choose from a list that includes the most common religions, “no religion” and “other”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Labour MPs call for Andy Burnham to restore aid spending target set by Brown
stories.summary: Thinktank urges prospective prime minister to reclaim UK’s role as an international leader on development Influential backbenchers are calling on Andy Burnham to reclaim Labour’s leadership on international development and chart a course back to spending 0.7% of national income on overseas aid. In a collection of essays to be published soon by the New Economics Foundation (NEF) thinktank, MPs lay out proposals for a Burnham-led government to rethink foreign policy. Continue reading...

stories.title: Panino police: packed lunch bans enrage Italians at pricey beach clubs
stories.summary: Child who ate smuggled-in homemade sandwich kicks off latest skirmish over private resorts As lunchtime approaches at Il Tirreno, a private beach club in Montalto di Castro on the Lazio coast, Beatrice Bordo, sitting in the shade of a blue umbrella, unwraps a slice of pizza. A pranzo al sacco , or packed lunch, has become the latest skirmish in Italy’s long-running beach disputes after a woman was confronted over the clandestine consumption of a homemade sandwich at a private establishment in Pu

stories.title: Man arrested on suspicion of Ann Widdecombe’s murder is released
stories.summary: The 26-year-old man is no longer part of the investigation, Devon and Cornwall police say A 26-year-old man who was arrested on suspicion of the murder of the former MP Ann Widdecombe has been released from custody and is no longer part of the investigation, Devon and Cornwall police said early on Saturday. In a statement, Assistant Chief Constable Matt Longman said: “Our priority remains identifying those responsible and ensuring that all available evidence is thoroughly examined. Continue read

stories.title: John Humphrys criticises successors on ‘irritating’ Today programme
stories.summary: Ex-presenter of BBC Radio 4 show complains in Guardian of ‘gratuitous gratitude’ and ‘gushing’ between host and guest For more than three decades, John Humphrys delighted and infuriated listeners in equal measure as he confronted the nation’s politicians in his trademark, pointed style on the Today programme. Now a listener himself, the former presenter of the BBC’s flagship radio news show is just as pointed in his assessment of the current incarnation of the programme. His verdict? It’s irrita

stories.summary: Officers are carrying out ‘numerous inquiries’ but 26-year-old, arrested on Friday afternoon, is no longer part of the investigation A 26-year-old man arrested on suspicion of murdering the former MP Ann Widdecombe has been released from custody and is no longer part of the investigation, Devon and Cornwall police said early on Saturday. stories.title: Once again Trump brought his wrecking ball to the Nato summit, and once again the alliance survived. But for how long? | Paul Taylor
stories.summary: As the US president swung between threats to take Greenland and promises of help for Ukraine, pledges of a ‘stronger Nato’ were lost in the wind Nato leaders survived another nerve-racking summit with Donald Trump and the 77-year-old defence alliance lives to fight another day, proving its durability against Atlantic storms. But it will never feel safe as long as the unpredictable, vengeful and ruthlessly transactional US president is in the White House. As usual, Trump stole all the headlines a

stories.title: Protests engulf Indian state after rape and murder of 11-year-old girl
stories.summary: Innocent man lynched by mob in West Bengal as police killing of suspect further escalates tensions Protests have engulfed the Indian state of West Bengal after the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl, the subsequent lynching of an innocent man and the police killing of one of the accused. Outrage erupted on Sunday after the body of a missing girl was recovered from a pond in a town just outside the state capital, Kolkata. Continue reading...

stories.title: Nigerian military rescues 46 abducted school children
stories.summary: The children were abducted some two months ago at three schools in the town of Orire in Nigeria's southwestern Oyo state.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over stealing 'trade secrets'
stories.summary: Apple has accused the company behind ChatGPT and two of its former employees of misappropriating its trade secrets to benefit OpenAI's foray into consumer hardware.

stories.title: Cuba suffers second nationwide blackout in five days
stories.summary: Cuba's electricity grid has crumbled amid a six-month US fuel blockade and already-dilapidated energy infrastructure.

stories.title: US wants Iran to pledge to halt Hormuz attacks — officials
stories.summary: Washington reportedly wants Tehran to publicly vow to end attacks on vessels in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Donald Trump has threatened to "completely decimate" Iran should it attempt to assassinate him. DW has more.

stories.title: Could AI help al-Qaida and other groups plan terror attacks?
stories.summary: Followers of extremist groups regularly ask how AI can help them plan terrorist attacks. A new study suggests that about one-third of AI chatbots might help them, if asked the right way.

stories.title: Germany news: Third summer heat wave looms
stories.summary: Germany is in for another scorching week, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say. Meanwhile, a man has taken a woman hostage at a Berlin supermarket. DW has more

stories.title: New dinosaur species with neck as long as cricket pitch discovered in Thailand
stories.summary: The plant-eating dinosaur, named Uragasaurus kalasinensis, is thought to have lived about 150 million years ago.

stories.title: Twelve men and 12 women enter a Buddhist temple - will the monks help them find love?
stories.summary: Dating can be hard in South Korea. A group of monks hope a 30-hour rural retreat can help.

stories.title: Watch: Inside the 30-hour dating retreat run by monks
stories.summary: Forget coffee or drinks, the latest dating trend in South Korea takes place somewhere very different, a Buddhist temple.

stories.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was not intended target, DHS says
stories.summary: Immigration agents were looking for a different person when they shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo during a traffic stop, officials say.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI, its employees claiming theft of trade secrets
stories.summary: Apple said in a Friday lawsuit that OpenAI’s nascent hardware business is “rotten to its core.”

stories.title: The photo that shows who's really on top in Le Pen's party
stories.summary: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.

stories.title: Meet the crypto billionaires building a world where money buys you a vote
stories.summary: They think democracy has run its course - so they're setting up their own nations instead.

stories.title: At Canada's biggest rodeo, the starting gun is fired in the fight over Alberta separation
stories.summary: Canadian unity takes centre stage at the Calgary Stampede as fears of a Brexit-style upset looms over the October vote on Alberta's future.

stories.title: 'Devastating' evidence against Charlie Kirk murder suspect laid out in court
stories.summary: Prosecutors build a vivid picture of the suspect's movements as they seek to try the 23-year-old.

stories.title: Meta pulls new AI image feature after days of backlash
stories.summary: Meta's release this week of an AI feature that let people alter Instagram content drew swift blowback.

stories.title: Japanese school teaches children how to stay safe around wild bears
stories.summary: Children at a primary school in central Japan are taking part in a bear awareness programme, learning how to safely coexist with wild bears as sightings remain common in the area.

stories.title: Small plane crash in Bahamas kills 10 people
stories.summary: The country's prime minister called the crash, on the country's independence day, a tragedy.

stories.title: US wants Iran to pledge to stop shooting at ships in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Vice-President JD Vance is among US officials expected to take part in negotiations resuming on Saturday in Oman.

stories.title: Spain fans celebrate World Cup semi-final place after Belgium win
stories.summary: Spain celebrated a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium on 10 July as fans filled Madrid to cheer La Roja into the FIFA World Cup semi-finals.

stories.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
stories.summary: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

stories.title: Georgia left off NATO summit partner list as critics decry isolation
stories.summary: The Georgian government is facing accusations of international isolation after not being invited to the NATO summit in Turkey, the first time a key partner has been excluded from the alliance's agenda.

stories.title: US and Iran trade escalating strikes as supreme leader is buried after days-long funeral
stories.summary: Renewed attacks the largest since an interim memorandum was signed this June, with Trump saying fragile truce is ‘over’ The US and Iran traded retaliatory strikes on Thursday as US president Donald Trump threatened to escalate the conflict unless Iran stopped attacking ships in the strait of Hormuz. Iran responded to the latest round of attacks by targeting US-allied Kuwait and Qatar and accused the US of striking near its sole nuclear power plant. Continue reading...

stories.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

stories.title: Weather tracker: Typhoon leaves people stranded on rooftops in China
stories.summary: Eleven reported dead as flooding also brings danger of snakes, while buildings collapse in Mumbai amid heavy rain As the first typhoon to make landfall in China for the 2026 season, Maysak has caused devastating damage in southern and central regions. The Guangxi region received intense downpours of up to 280mm in 12 hours, causing rivers to swell and dam walls to break. stories.title: Journalist Richard Guilliatt resigns from Walkley awards board after critic wins reporting prize

stories.title: Why pay a premium fee for a service that isn’t? How the nationwide outage could hurt Telstra
stories.summary: The telco has long banked on its reputation as having the most stable, widest mobile telco coverage. stories.title: Test runs and a shock-absorbing cage: how Bayeux tapestry was moved to UK
stories.summary: Medieval artwork safely delivered to British Museum before going on display from September in tightly controlled conditions Bayeux tapestry crosses Channel in dead of night The Bayeux tapestry has survived myriad perils, from cathedral fires to its potential destruction for use as wagon covers. Now, with the embroidery about to be displayed in a blockbuster London exhibition , experts must contend with a host of more insidious dangers. stories.title: ‘The worst possible combination’: what has caused Spain’s deadly wildfires?
stories.summary: Blaze made worse by heat, strong wind and plentiful dry vegetation amid hollowing out of rural population Wildfire in southern Spain kills at least 12 amid heatwave As firefighters struggle to quell the flames ravaging southern Spain and doctors treat the injured, a horrific picture of the fallout is emerging. stories.title: High court rejects most of ‘dieselgate’ claims brought by 1.6m UK car owners
stories.summary: Carmakers welcome ruling against suit claiming manufacturers including Nissan, Ford and Peugeot fitted devices to defeat emission tests Car manufacturers have welcomed a high court verdict that rejected most of the allegations in a “dieselgate” claim brought on behalf of 1.6 million UK owners of polluting cars. In her judgment, Lady Justice Cockerill said that “in the majority of instances, the court found that the relevant strategy did not constitute a prohibited defeat device” – software that

stories.title: Fast-spreading wildfire kills at least 12 in southern Spain
stories.summary: Twenty-three people missing and four Britons thought to be among those who died trying to flee Almería blaze ‘I had an incredible escape’: British woman tells of close encounter with wildfire At least 12 people have been killed and 23 are unaccounted for after one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires broke out in the south-eastern province of Almería as the country endures its second heatwave of the summer. The regional government of Andalucía said the victims, four of whom are believed to be British,

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: The US’s 250th anniversary, the funeral of Ali Khamenei, another brutal heatwave in Europe and the World Cup – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
stories.summary: Dieter Schwarz made his fortune from supermarkets, becoming Germany's wealthiest person. Now he wants to take on Google, Microsoft and Amazon and an entire region stands to benefit.

stories.title: Vegan pregnancy tests and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Keeping cool in the heat with a millennia-old tradition in India, a German company making animal-free medical tests, and building from bottles in Mozambique.

stories.title: China retrieves booster in reusable rocket breakthrough
stories.summary: China has joined the small group of space powers that can now recover an orbital-class booster rocket. Developers of the landing method turned to a striking alternative to that used by existing players such as SpaceX.

stories.title: Vietnam's baby bonuses unlikely to stop the aging clock
stories.summary: Vietnam has joined Southeast Asia's push to raise birth rates, but experts say the greater challenge lies in adapting economies and welfare systems to an aging population.

stories.title: When London stank to high heaven
stories.summary: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

stories.title: Passenger partly sucked from Ryanair plane during flight
stories.summary: A man was nearly sucked out the cabin window on a Ryanair flight from Greece to Germany. An initial loud bang was followed by a desperate rescue effort to save the passenger.

stories.title: Spain: Wildfire kills several amid heat wave
stories.summary: The victims include British and other foreign nationals, authorities have said. The Spanish royal family and Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez expressed their deep sorrow over the loss of life.

stories.title: UK police arrest murder suspect in Ann Widdecombe's death
stories.summary: Police in southwest England say they have arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murdering politician Ann Widdecombe. Based on initial inquiries, they were not treating the case as terrorism or politically motivated.

stories.title: Germany: Bundestag passes controversial healthcare reform
stories.summary: The cost-cutting package has passed Germany's parliament despite fierce criticism from the opposition. Meanwhile, Alexander Zverev has become the first German man to reach the Wimbledon final since 1995. DW has more.

stories.title: EU threatens Meta with hefty fine over 'addictive design'
stories.summary: A preliminary investigation by the EU found that the social media giant is not doing enough to protect children who use its platforms. Meta rejects the accusations, which could cost it 6% of its annual revenue in fines.

stories.title: Spain wildfire near Almeria kills 11 and sparks major evacuations
stories.summary: Spain is battling a deadly wildfire in Los Gallardos, near Almeria, where at least 11 people had died by 10 July after flames swept through a semi-arid area near the Sierra de los Filabres.

stories.title: Spain: Nine injured in fourth San Fermín bull run in Pamplona
stories.summary: The fourth San Fermin bull run was once again a fast and dangerous dash along the cobbled streets of Pamplona.

stories.title: East Asia braces for destructive typhoon as landslides kill 15 in Philippines
stories.summary: Heading for Taiwan and south-eastern China, the 1,000 km-wide Bavi is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades.

stories.title: Boxy forms and odd lights: US declassifies more UFO files
stories.summary: The US government published a fourth instalment of unresolved cases, promising to release more files on a rolling basis.

stories.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket booster recovery
stories.summary: China successfully recaptured the first stage of a rocket after a launch on Friday in a breakthrough for the country's space program, state media said. The first stage of a Long March-10B rocket separated from the second stage after liftoff and returned to a platform in the sea.

stories.title: Zulu king expresses regret after video captures tirade against his wife
stories.summary: In the footage widely shared in South Africa, King Misuzulu threatens to physically assault his wife and accuses her of cheating.

stories.title: Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say
stories.summary: The man's wife held onto his legs for around five minutes to stop him from being sucked out, officials say.

stories.title: Watch: What links a bomb in Monaco and a shooting in Ukraine?
stories.summary: A Ukrainian intelligence agent who confessed to killing the woman suspected of trying to assassinate a multimillionaire in Monaco, has now claimed he did not pull the trigger.

stories.title: Crews drain Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool again as part of troubled Trump revamp
stories.summary: Trump directed that the bottom be painted what he called “American flag blue” but problems began quickly after the initial work was finished.

stories.summary: A spanyolok vezettek, a belgák egyetlen helyzetüket kihasználva egyenlítettek, de a hajrában a spanyolok Courtois lecserélése után 2-1-re nyertek a futball-világbajnokság negyeddöntőjében.

stories.title: Courtois: Simán tudtam volna a kapuban maradni, de az edző azt mondta, ha nem vagy százszázalékos, lecseréllek
stories.summary: A belga válogatott 2-1-re kikapott a spanyoloktól a vb negyeddöntőjében, a győztes gól előtt a cserekapus Senne Lammens hibázott nagyot.

stories.title: Elég megadni az épület címét, pár fotót, és az MI meg is tervezi az állványzatot
stories.summary: A Deutsche Welle riportjából kiderül, hogyan gyorsítja fel az építkezéseket a mesterséges intelligencia.

stories.summary: Exclusive: PM-in-waiting says party must ‘do better’ in approach to Middle East and he will put more pressure on Israel Analysis: Burnham’s apology marks ‘reset moment’ as Labour seeks to win back progressive voters Andy Burnham has apologised for Labour’s initial response to Israel’s military action in Gaza, saying the party “didn’t get it right” and needs to “do better” under his leadership signalling a significant shift in the UK’s approach to the Middle East. stories.title: Furore in Nigeria over fake federal agency set up in government HQ
stories.summary: President orders investigation after fictitious body given funding, triggering renewed scrutiny of alleged corruption A fictitious federal entity that was allocated 1.3bn naira (£700,000) in Nigeria’s 2026 budget has precipitated a political storm in Africa’s largest democracy in the run-up to a general election set for January. The fake agency came to light last October when Femi Gbajabiamila, the president’s chief of staff, wrote to the police alleging that his signature, along with official s

stories.title: Mexico investigates whether US lied about role in capture of drug lord
stories.summary: Reporting suggests FBI involved in seizure of Ismael ‘El Mayo’ Zambada García from Mexican territory in 2024 Mexico has launched an investigation into whether the US lied about its involvement in the capture and secretive transfer of a top Sinaloa cartel member in 2024, in what would be a potential violation of the country’s sovereignty. The US has long denied it played any role in the operation to detain the drug lord Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada García, a founder of the Sinaloa cartel, inside Mexi

stories.title: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
stories.summary: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

stories.title: Mosque replica and anti-immigration signs appear on Northern Ireland bonfire
stories.summary: A loyalist bonfire in Moygashel, Northern Ireland displays a mosque replica and anti-immigration signs ahead of Orange Order celebrations.

stories.summary: Since returning to the White House, Trump has taken repeated measures to put his name and image in official spaces, an abrupt break with American political tradition.

stories.title: Marine Le Pen ‘wants to talk politics’, but can she drown out the legal noise?
stories.summary: The French far-right leader’s presidential campaign is clouded in uncertainty under the shadow of an embezzlement conviction Marine Le Pen ’s decision to run for French president in 2027, despite her legal woes, has drawn comparisons from her opponents to Donald Trump. Just as the US president felt his voter base cared little about legal investigations against him, the French far-right leader shrugged off the leftwing protesters who shouted “criminal!” as she launched her presidential campaign a

stories.title: Venomous snakes escape breeding farms in southern China during flooding
stories.summary: Local media in Hengzhou report king ratsnakes and cobras among hundreds in flood waters caused by typhoon Maysak Hundreds of snakes, including cobras, have escaped from flooded breeding farms in southern China as severe storms continue to batter parts of the country. State media reported that a snake farm in Hengzhou, in Guangxi Zhuang autonomous region, was hit by flood waters after days of heavy rainfall caused by typhoon Maysak , prompting warnings for nearby residents. Continue reading...

stories.title: Species’ ingenious survival strategies no match for human destruction, red list reveals
stories.summary: Newly endangered animals include desert frogs and snails in extreme ocean depths, both threatened by mining Life has colonised every corner of the planet by evolving ingenious survival strategies but these are increasingly being overwhelmed by destructive human activities, this year’s red list of endangered species has revealed. Many snails, limpets and clams have adapted to life at crushing depths in the oceans on hydrothermal vents where water temperatures can reach 450C (842F). But an assessm

stories.title: LGBTQ+ cruise ship refused entry to Egypt days after Turkey turned it away
stories.summary: Scarlet Lady’s 2,000 passengers told of change as one of those onboard says they will ‘sparkle and spend elsewhere’ An LGBTQ+ cruise ship blocked from Turkish waters this week has been refused entry into Egypt. The Scarlet Lady’s 2,000 passengers, including the Broadway performer Patti LuPone , woke on Thursday morning to find a note placed under their cabin doors informing them that the ship was urgently looking for alternative ports. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tehran launches more strikes as Israel warns it is ready to strike Iran again ‘with even greater force’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. US and Iran trade most intense strikes since ceasefire extended Kuwait’s foreign ministry has issued a statement condemning the Iranian attacks against the country. It reads almost identical to the statement issued yesterday, although emphasises Kuwait’s sovereignty is “a red line” . “The state of Kuwait reserves its full rights to take all necessary measures to protect its security and preserve its sovereignty,” it said. Continue reading...

Ahmad Esleem was shot in the head on Wednesday when an aid convoy stopped because of a breakdown to one truck soon after enter

stories.title: ‘A lot of red flags’: plans for New Zealand’s first datacentre spark concern as locals demand greater transparency
stories.summary: Plans to build a NZ$3.5bn datacentre in Makarewa in the country’s south has drawn concern about electricity and water use, and potential noise pollution People living near the site of New Zealand’s first planned AI datacentre are calling for more transparency about the project, especially about how the centre’s huge electricity and water use and potential noise pollution could affect them. Singapore-based company Datagrid has secured approval to build a NZ$3.5bn (US$2bn) AI datacentre on a 49-he

stories.title: Heatwave across western Europe – in pictures
stories.summary: Western Europe has been scorched by its hottest June on record, scientists have said, as the UK enters its third heatwave of the year and wildfires ravage France and Spain Western Europe records hottest-ever June as heatwaves intensify Continue reading...

stories.title: South Korea chip maker SK hynix rides AI boom raising $26.5bn in huge US listing
stories.summary: SK hynix, a supplier of advanced memory chips, has seen profits skyrocket thanks to the global race to build AI datacentres South Korean chip maker SK hynix set pricing for its mega US listing on Friday, aiming to raise $26.5bn as it takes advantage of the AI boom in what will be one of the world’s biggest ever stock sales. The Asian semiconductor giant plans to issue the equivalent of about 18m shares on Wall Street’s tech-heavy Nasdaq index later in the day. Continue reading...

stories.title: Developing countries spend more repaying foreign debt than on education, UN reveals
stories.summary: Unesco report shows children lost out to servicing debt in 113 countries, with 18 spending five times more on loans Most developing countries spent less on education than they did repaying debt last year, according to the UN, at the same time as global aid to education is predicted to decline by up to 30%. stories.summary: A kétszeres Oscar-díjas színész bevallása szerint az első szerelme mindig a zene volt.

stories.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

stories.summary: The Welsh singer was best known for the 1980s rock opera hit, along with other songs, including "It's a Heartache."

stories.title: How do Germans cope with having Nazi grandparents?
stories.summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

stories.title: California’s largest lake is turning to dust
stories.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for people living near its shores. Authorities are working to stabilize the lakebed and lock down the dust. But for some families, it's too late.

stories.summary: Robert Wun presents his 'Childsplay' couture collection during Paris Haute Couture Week, blending dramatic designs with childhood-inspired creativity.

stories.title: At least seven killed in fresh Russian strikes on Ukraine as NATO leaders meet in Turkey

stories.summary: Trump had said that the ceasefire with Iran is "over" after Washington launched strikes on the country overnight, to which Iran responded by targeting US bases in the Gulf.

stories.title: ‘Software glitch’ blamed for mass outage – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Telstra outage: Telco apologises for major time-keeping issue that hit mobiles, trains and triple-zero calls Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Telstra outage reported to be cause of Victoria train stoppage There is a long list of cancelled trains on the official V/line site, but the TL/DR is that Victoria’s regional train network has ground to a halt. Due to a radio network fault affecting the network, services are currently unable to operate …

stories.title: UK judges begin hearing appeal over Trinidad and Tobago anti-gay law
stories.summary: Activist is challenging ruling last year that restored colonial-era homophobic law against same-sex intimacy Some of the UK’s top judges are hearing arguments over whether a Trinidad and Tobago court had the legal right to overturn a 2018 ruling to remove colonial-era homophobic laws that criminalise anal sex between consenting men. The country’s “ buggery law ”, often referred to as its “sodomy” law, was created in 1925 and was written into Trinidad and Tobago’s 1986 Sexual Offences Act . In 20

stories.title: Pakistan locates wreckage of Boeing 737 cargo plane that went missing off coast
stories.summary: Early flight data shows K2 Airways plane crashed into sea with five crew on board south-west of Karachi Pakistan has located the wreckage of a Boeing cargo plane, the country’s airports authority said, adding that rescuers were searching for the five crew members on board when the aircraft went missing. The plane was approaching Karachi from Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates when radar showed it “rapidly descending” on Tuesday evening after reporting a “navigational system issue”, according to

stories.title: US charges Indian criminal gang leader with organising murder of Canadian Sikh activist
stories.summary: Lawrence Bishnoi, who is in prison in India, is accused of orchestrating assassination of Hardeep Singh Nijjar in 2023 US and Canadian authorities say they have “dismantled” the leadership of a notorious Indian criminal group, charging dozens of operatives who have “inflicted pain and cruelty on people, victims around the globe”, including a high-profile murder in Canada that strained diplomatic relations between Canada and India. At a press conference on Tuesday, members of the FBI and Canada’s

stories.title: The Australian tree supercharging global wildfires
stories.summary: As Europe experiences another summer of extreme heat and wildfire warnings, one tree imported from Australia is coming under renewed scrutiny across the world: eucalyptus.

stories.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
stories.summary: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

stories.title: France's far right set to improve ties with Germany
stories.summary: France’s right-wing populist Marine Le Pen is running for President. Her chances aren’t bad. Her party, the RN, is already reaching out to Germany.

stories.title: Germany reports 'shock' rise in drug deaths among under-30s
stories.summary: The number of drug-related deaths remains at a record high in Germany. And the victims are getting younger and younger. Many deaths are related to new and increasingly dangerous substances such as synthetic opioids.

stories.title: Egypt's wrath after World Cup exit: 'The match was rigged'
stories.summary: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

stories.title: Erdogan gives world leaders a ceremonial welcome ahead of NATO summit
stories.summary: NATO leaders including Trump and Zelenskyy arrive in Ankara for a state dinner hosted by Erdogan ahead of key summit talks in Turkey.

stories.title: Gulf states brace again for fiery US-Iran showdown as tensions spiral
stories.summary: From the front lines of the US-Iran confrontation, the Gulf nations have again urged both sides, with strategic restraint, not to abandon their negotiations, while Tehran is telegraphing that the Strait of Hormuz is now Iran's.

stories.title: Thousands embark on Bosnia peace march to remember Srebrenica victims
stories.summary: Over 6,500 people took part in a peace march to remember the victims of the Srebrenica genocide and the survivors who walked for days to save their lives.

stories.title: Rescuers search for five missing people after deadly landslide in India's Kerala state
stories.summary: Experts say human-caused climate change is intensifying South Asia’s monsoons, which traditionally run from June to September and again from October to December.

stories.title: Venezuela calls for release of frozen assets to help earthquake recovery efforts
stories.summary: Meanwhile, the United Nations launched an urgent appeal on Wednesday to raise €259 million for relief operations.

stories.title: Trump skips new Air Force One on return from Turkey NATO summit
stories.summary: Asked during a press conference whether the change was for security reasons related to Iran, Trump sidestepped the question by talking about previous alleged assassination attempts by Tehran.

stories.title: A rock star welcome and human rights protests to greet India’s prime minister Narendra Modi in Australia
stories.summary: Amnesty International says Albanese has opportunity to ‘reaffirm mutual commitment’ to rule of law as thousands in diaspora expected at stadium event Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Indian prime minister Narendra Modi’s first visit to Australia in three years has sparked calls for the federal government to raise human rights concerns, as excitement builds within the nation’s Indian diaspora. Modi’s visit begi

stories.title: Attacks on Kyiv ahead of NATO summit: What is Putin's goal?
stories.summary: Kyiv suffered heavy Russian strikes ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara. Experts say Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to send a message.

stories.title: ‘Living like this is agony’: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in six months
stories.summary: Impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US imposed a blockade in January Cuba on Monday suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, the state electricity company said. The impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US president, Donald Trump, imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU rejects suspending biometric border controls despite 20 ‘difficult spots’
stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda. Catnip, or Nepeta cataria , is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this has not previously been commercialised. Continue

stories.title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president in 2027 – video
stories.summary: Speaking in a prime-time interview on TF1 TV on Tuesday, the French far-right leader Marine Le Pen announced she will run for president in 2027 after an appeal court shortened her ban on holding public office. On Tuesday, the Paris appeals court upheld Le Pen's conviction for misusing European parliament funds but reduced the ban on running for office, clearing the way for the 57-year-old to stand in next year's election Continue reading...

stories.title: Why migrants come to Germany for work and then leave again
stories.summary: Germany has long attracted skilled workers to its labor market. The country has been much less successful at retaining them. So what is Germany doing wrong?

stories.title: Germany: Record debt and a seismic policy shift
stories.summary: The German government's draft budget for 2027 includes more money for the military and less for everything else, while debt continues to grow rapidly.

stories.title: Tibetan activists detained outside Chinese Embassy in New Delhi
stories.summary: Tibetan Youth Congress activists are detained by police during a 'Free Tibet' protest outside the Chinese Embassy in New Delhi on 7 July 2026.

stories.title: Dowry murders in India no longer spark public anger or debate, study finds
stories.summary: Thousands of women are killed in dowry disputes each year, despite the practice being banned in 1961 Dowry deaths in India no longer provoke the public anger they once did, despite thousands of women’s lives still being lost every year, according to new research. The killings – women who are murdered or driven to suicide following dowry disputes between families – have also faded from political debate, despite an increase in cases. Continue reading...

stories.title: Wildfires in southern Europe wreak havoc – in pictures
stories.summary: Wildfires raged across southern Europe, forcing thousands of people to evacuate their homes and prompting officials to ban spectators from a stage of the storied Tour de France cycling race Continue reading...

stories.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
stories.summary: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron visits Syria, 18 months after Assad's fall
stories.summary: The French president landed in Damascus on Monday evening. He's the first EU leader to visit the country since Bashar Assad's sudden downfall and the rise of former militant turned President Ahmed al-Sharaa.
